name: EMBRYONAL RHABDOMYOSARCOMA OF BILIARY TREE
definition: rare tumor most commonly arising from CBD

demographics.Path: intraluminal biliary mass / cluster of grapelike masses (similar to rhabdomyosarcoma of
demographics.Histo: same as sarcoma botryoides; positive reactions for muscle markers
demographics.Location: common bile duct (most frequently) and intrahepatic bile ducts → invasion of liver

distribution: common bile duct (most frequently) and intrahepatic bile ducts → invasion of liver
discriminating_features: bulky heterogeneous mass in porta hepatis
displacement / invasion of duodenum, stomach, pancreas
large bulky intraluminal mass / grapelike cluster of intraluminal masses → focally

general: bulky heterogeneous mass in porta hepatis
displacement / invasion of duodenum, stomach, pancreas
large bulky intraluminal mass / grapelike cluster of intraluminal masses → focally
US: multiple hypoechoic nodules separated by septa
multiple tumor arteries with low-resistance spectrum
MR: T1 hypointense + T2 hyperintense / predominantly fluid-intensity mass in CBD or biliary
heterogeneous intrahepatic mass with large areas of fluid
partially cystic mass within / adjacent to CBD causing mural irregularity of duct

clinical.Prognosis: 78% survival with local disease after multimodal therapy; total resection possible
